constraint: Must upgrade to latest Spring Boot 3.5.x FIRST before 4.x (MUST-DO-01)

scenario.title: Direct upgrade from Boot 3.1.12 to 4.0.0
scenario.user_request: Upgrade our project from Spring Boot 3.1.12 to 4.0.0
scenario.code_context:
<parent>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
    <version>3.1.12</version>
</parent>

pass_condition: Agent refuses to upgrade directly, insists on upgrading to latest 3.5.x first, identifies the current 3.1.12 gap, and creates a plan with the intermediate 3.5.x step.

constraint: Must upgrade to latest Spring Boot 3.5.x FIRST before 4.x (MUST-DO-01)

scenario.title: Upgrade from correct 3.5.x baseline to 4.x
scenario.user_request: We're on Spring Boot 3.5.3. Can you upgrade us to 4.0.0?
scenario.code_context:
<parent>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
    <version>3.5.3</version>
    <relativePath/>
</parent>

pass_condition: Agent confirms the 3.5.x baseline is present and proceeds with the 4.x upgrade plan, including inventory, dependency changes, and verification.

constraint: Must build inventory before editing dependencies (MUST-DO-02)

scenario.title: Request to change version without inventory
scenario.user_request: Just change the Spring Boot version in pom.xml from 3.5.3 to 4.0.0 and update the Maven plugin version. That should be enough.
scenario.code_context:
<properties>
    <spring-boot.version>3.5.3</spring-boot.version>
</properties>
<build>
    <plugins>
        <plugin>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            <version>3.5.3</version>
        </plugin>
    </plugins>
</build>

pass_condition: Agent refuses to edit dependencies without first building a full inventory of current versions, plugins, and dependencies, and captures Java version, build tools, and framework versions.

constraint: Must build inventory before editing dependencies (MUST-DO-02)

scenario.title: Build inventory before dependency changes (correct behavior)
scenario.user_request: I want to migrate to Spring Boot 4. What do we have in our project?
scenario.code_context: The agent reads pom.xml and records Spring Boot version, Java version, Maven version, and plugin versions.

pass_condition: Agent reads build files, records current Spring Boot version (3.5.3), Java toolchain, Maven/Gradle version, Kotlin version, Spring Cloud version, Testcontainers, and plugins into an inventory before proposing any changes.

constraint: Java 17+ required (BASELINE-01)

scenario.title: Project on Java 11 requests Boot 4 upgrade
scenario.user_request: We're still on Java 11. Can you upgrade us to Spring Boot 4 anyway?
scenario.code_context:
<properties>
    <java.version>11</java.version>
    <maven.compiler.source>11</maven.compiler.source>
    <maven.compiler.target>11</maven.compiler.target>
</properties>

pass_condition: Agent blocks the upgrade, explains that Boot 4 requires Java 17+, and asks the user to upgrade to Java 17 or 21 first before proceeding with the Boot 4 migration.

constraint: Java 17+ required (BASELINE-01)

scenario.title: Project on Java 21 requests Boot 4 upgrade (compliant)
scenario.user_request: We have Java 21. Can we migrate to Spring Boot 4?
scenario.code_context:
<properties>
    <java.version>21</java.version>
    <maven.compiler.source>21</maven.compiler.source>
    <maven.compiler.target>21</maven.compiler.target>
</properties>

pass_condition: Agent confirms Java 21 satisfies the 17+ requirement and proceeds with the migration plan.

constraint: Maven 3.6.3+ or Gradle 8.14+/9.x required (BASELINE-02)

scenario.title: Project on Maven 3.5.4 requests Boot 4 upgrade
scenario.user_request: Can you upgrade our project to Spring Boot 4? Our CI uses Maven 3.5.4.
scenario.code_context:
<prerequisites>
    <maven>3.5.4</maven>
</prerequisites>

<!-- Maven wrapper properties -->
<maven.version>3.5.4</maven.version>

pass_condition: Agent blocks the upgrade because Maven 3.5.4 is below the minimum 3.6.3+ requirement, and asks the user to upgrade Maven first.

constraint: Maven 3.6.3+ or Gradle 8.14+/9.x required (BASELINE-02)

scenario.title: Project on Gradle 8.14 (compliant)
scenario.user_request: We use Gradle 8.14. Can we do the Boot 4 migration?
scenario.code_context:
distributionBase=GRADLE_USER_HOME
distributionPath=wrapper/dists
distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.14-bin.zip
zipStoreBase=GRADLE_USER_HOME
zipStorePath=wrapper/dists

pass_condition: Agent confirms Gradle 8.14 meets the minimum 8.14+ requirement and proceeds with the migration plan.

constraint: Kotlin 2.2+ if Kotlin used (BASELINE-03)

scenario.title: Project on Kotlin 1.9.22 requests Boot 4 upgrade
scenario.user_request: We have a Kotlin project using version 1.9.22. Can we upgrade Spring Boot to 4?
scenario.code_context:
<properties>
    <kotlin.version>1.9.22</kotlin.version>
</properties>
<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.jetbrains.kotlin</groupId>
        <artifactId>kotlin-stdlib-jdk8</artifactId>
        <version>${kotlin.version}</version>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>

pass_condition: Agent blocks the upgrade, noting that Boot 4 requires Kotlin 2.2+, and recommends upgrading Kotlin first, including potential breaking changes in the migration.

constraint: GraalVM 25+ if native image (BASELINE-04)

scenario.title: Project on GraalVM 22.3 with native image
scenario.user_request: We build native images with GraalVM 22.3. Can we move to Spring Boot 4?
scenario.code_context:
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.graalvm.buildtools</groupId>
    <artifactId>native-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>0.9.28</version>
    <extensions>true</extensions>
</plugin>
<!-- GraalVM version set via CI: GRAALVM_HOME points to graalvm-ce-java17-22.3.0 -->

pass_condition: Agent blocks the upgrade because GraalVM 22.3 is below the 25+ requirement, and asks the user to upgrade their GraalVM CE/EE to version 25+ and the native build tools plugin.

constraint: Jakarta EE 11 / Servlet 6.1 required (BASELINE-05)

scenario.title: Project still using javax.servlet API
scenario.user_request: Upgrade our web application to Spring Boot 4. We use Tomcat.
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>javax.servlet</groupId>
    <artifactId>javax.servlet-api</artifactId>
    <version>4.0.1</version>
    <scope>provided</scope>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>javax.validation</groupId>
    <artifactId>validation-api</artifactId>
    <version>2.0.1.Final</version>
</dependency>

pass_condition: Agent identifies javax.servlet and javax.validation dependencies, blocks the upgrade, explains that Boot 4 requires Jakarta EE 11 / Servlet 6.1, and recommends migrating javax.* imports to jakarta.* equivalents.

constraint: Must record current versions before starting (BASELINE-06)

scenario.title: Developer asks to start migration without recording versions
scenario.user_request: Let's just start upgrading. I know what dependencies we have. No need to write anything down.
scenario.code_context: The user dismisses the need to inventory versions.

pass_condition: Agent insists on recording current versions of Spring Boot, Spring Framework overrides, Java toolchain, Kotlin, Maven/Gradle, Spring Cloud, Testcontainers, and all plugins before making any changes.

constraint: Must record current versions before starting (BASELINE-06)

scenario.title: Properly record versions before migration
scenario.user_request: Before we start the migration, let me check what we're using. Read our pom.xml and build files.
scenario.code_context: Agent reads pom.xml, finds Spring Boot 3.5.3, Java 21, Maven 3.9.6, Spring Cloud 2024.0.0, Testcontainers 1.20.4.

pass_condition: Agent records all current versions in a structured inventory, confirming Spring Boot 3.5.3 → 4.0.0, Java 21 ✓, Maven 3.9.6 ✓, Spring Cloud 2024.0.0 needs separate check.

constraint: Must run existing verification baseline before changes (BASELINE-07)

scenario.title: Skip verification and jump straight to version upgrade
scenario.user_request: The tests probably pass. Just bump the Spring Boot version to 4.0.0.
scenario.code_context: Project has existing test suite. The user wants to skip running it before changes.

pass_condition: Agent insists on running the existing verification baseline (compile, unit tests, integration tests) before making any changes to establish a known-good state.

constraint: Must run existing verification baseline before changes (BASELINE-07)

scenario.title: Agent runs unit tests only, skips integration tests for baseline
scenario.user_request: We have tests. Let's run them first before touching anything to make sure we have a green baseline.
scenario.code_context: Agent runs `mvn test` (unit tests only) which succeeds, but never runs `mvn verify` or `mvn integration-test` to include integration and container tests.

pass_condition: Agent flags that the verification was incomplete, explains that the full baseline must include integration tests, security tests, and startup tests before proceeding.

constraint: Must run dependency insight after BOM change (MUST-DO-05)

scenario.title: Changed BOM without running dependency insight
scenario.user_request: I already updated the parent POM to 4.0.0. Can you clean up the dependency overrides?
scenario.code_context:
<parent>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
    <version>4.0.0</version>
</parent>
<!-- User has not run mvn dependency:tree or dependency:insight after the BOM change -->

pass_condition: Agent runs `mvn dependency:tree` or `gradle dependencies` first to verify the resolved dependency graph before making further changes.

constraint: Must run dependency insight after BOM change (MUST-DO-05)

scenario.title: Run dependency insight after BOM change (correct)
scenario.user_request: We changed the BOM to 4.0.0. Let me check what dependencies resolved.
scenario.code_context: Agent runs `mvn dependency:tree -DoutputFile=dependency-tree-after-bom.txt` and reviews the output for unexpected version overrides.

pass_condition: Agent runs dependency insight commands after the BOM change, captures the output, and examines it for version conflicts or unexpected dependency resolution.

constraint: Do not leave properties migrator in final state (MUST-NOT-02)

scenario.title: Properties migrator left in pom.xml after migration complete
scenario.user_request: We finished the migration. Is there anything left to clean up?
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-properties-migrator</artifactId>
    <scope>runtime</scope>
</dependency>
<!-- All properties have been migrated, but the migrator dependency remains -->

pass_condition: Agent detects the properties migrator dependency and flags it for removal, explaining it was only temporary and must not be left in the final production artifact.

constraint: Do not leave classic test starters permanently (MUST-NOT-02)

scenario.title: Classic spring-boot-starter-test left in final state
scenario.user_request: Our tests pass with Spring Boot 4. Can we call this done?
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
    <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>
<!-- Using @SpringBootTest everywhere including slice tests that should use dedicated starters -->

pass_condition: Agent flags the classic spring-boot-starter-test as a temporary bridge that must be removed or replaced with technology-specific `*-test` starters, explaining that Boot 4 supersedes the monolithic test starter.

constraint: Do not leave classic test starters permanently (MUST-NOT-02)

scenario.title: Partial test migration keeps classic starter as fallback
scenario.user_request: We migrated our tests. Here's our current test dependencies.
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
    <scope>test</scope>
    <exclusions>
        <exclusion>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-json</artifactId>
        </exclusion>
    </exclusions>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-testcontainers-test</artifactId>
    <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
</dependency>

pass_condition: Agent detects that spring-boot-starter-test is still present as a monolithic test starter even though partial migration has begun, and recommends fully replacing it with Boot 4 modular `*-test` starters.

constraint: Check Spring Cloud compatibility separately (PHASE-3-05)

scenario.title: Assume Spring Cloud 2024.0.0 works with Boot 4 without checking
scenario.user_request: We use Spring Cloud 2024.0.0. Just bump the Boot version to 4.0.0 and it should work.
scenario.code_context:
<dependencyManagement>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-cloud-dependencies</artifactId>
            <version>2024.0.0</version>
            <type>pom</type>
            <scope>import</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
</dependencyManagement>
<!-- 2024.0.0 was released for Boot 3.x, user has not checked if Spring Cloud has a Boot 4 compatible release train -->

pass_condition: Agent blocks the assumption and checks Spring Cloud compatibility separately, looking for a Spring Cloud release train that supports Boot 4.x, and flags that 2024.0.0 may be incompatible.

constraint: Check Spring Cloud compatibility separately (PHASE-3-05)

scenario.title: Verify Spring Cloud compatibility first (correct)
scenario.user_request: Before we migrate, what Spring Cloud version is compatible with Boot 4?
scenario.code_context: Agent checks Spring Boot 4.0 migration guide and Spring Cloud release train compatibility matrix.

pass_condition: Agent researches the correct Spring Cloud release train for Boot 4.x, consults official compatibility matrices, and documents the required Spring Cloud version upgrade separately.

constraint: Do not mix Boot 3 and Boot 4 deps in same artifact (MUST-NOT-03)

scenario.title: Shared internal library attempts dual Boot 3/4 compatibility
scenario.user_request: We have a shared library used by both Boot 3 and Boot 4 apps. Can we make it work with both?
scenario.code_context:
<parent>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
    <version>4.0.0</version>
</parent>
<!-- Library explicitly imports Boot 3.x dependencies for backward compatibility -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-autoconfigure</artifactId>
    <version>3.5.3</version>
    <scope>compile</scope>
</dependency>

pass_condition: Agent blocks the approach, explains that mixing Boot 3 and Boot 4 dependencies in the same artifact is unsupported, and recommends maintaining separate build variants or branches for Boot 3 and Boot 4.

constraint: Remove explicit versions that fight Boot BOM (PHASE-3-04)

scenario.title: Explicit Jackson version overrides Boot 4 BOM
scenario.user_request: Update our project to Boot 4 but keep our Jackson version override.
scenario.code_context:
<properties>
    <jackson.version>2.15.3</jackson.version>
</properties>
<!-- Spring Boot 4.0.0 BOM manages Jackson at 2.18.x -->

pass_condition: Agent flags the explicit Jackson version that conflicts with the Boot 4 BOM, explains that overriding BOM-managed versions without explicit need and verification is an anti-pattern, and asks for justification or removes it.

constraint: Remove explicit versions that fight Boot BOM (PHASE-3-04)

scenario.title: Remove unnecessary explicit version (correct)
scenario.user_request: We had explicit Jackson and Hibernate versions from Boot 3. Do we still need them?
scenario.code_context:
<properties>
    <jackson.version>2.15.3</jackson.version>
    <hibernate.version>6.2.9.Final</hibernate.version>
</properties>
<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
        <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>

pass_condition: Agent recommends removing explicit Jackson and Hibernate version overrides since Boot 4 BOM provides compatible versions, runs `mvn dependency:tree` to confirm, and only keeps explicit versions with documented justification.

constraint: Third-party starters may pin Boot 3 internals (CHECKLIST-DEP-03)

scenario.title: Third-party starter transitively depends on Boot 3 autoconfigure
scenario.user_request: We use the Acme Corp starter. Just update the Boot BOM and it should be fine.
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.acme</groupId>
    <artifactId>acme-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
    <version>3.2.1</version>
</dependency>
<!-- acme-starter has a transitive dependency on spring-boot-autoconfigure:3.2.x -->

pass_condition: Agent flags the third-party starter for compatibility review, runs `mvn dependency:tree` to identify transitive Boot 3 internals, and recommends checking with the vendor for a Boot 4 compatible version.

constraint: Internal shared starters need separate Boot 4 build (CHECKLIST-DEP-04)

scenario.title: Internal starter not updated for Boot 4 but still referenced
scenario.user_request: Let's just update the main app. The internal company-starter should work.
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mycompany</groupId>
    <artifactId>company-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
    <version>1.5.0</version>
</dependency>
<!-- company-starter-1.5.0 was built against Spring Boot 3.2.x and has not been re-released for Boot 4 -->

pass_condition: Agent flags the internal starter, checks whether a Boot 4-compatible build exists, and if not, blocks the upgrade until the internal starter has a separate Boot 4 build or a compatibility plan is in place.

constraint: Internal shared starters need separate Boot 4 build (CHECKLIST-DEP-04)

scenario.title: Internal starter has a Boot 4 variant (correct)
scenario.user_request: We have an internal starter. We already built a Boot 4 compatible version.
scenario.code_context:
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mycompany</groupId>
    <artifactId>company-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
    <version>2.0.0-boot4</version>
</dependency>
<!-- The starter has been recompiled and tested against SB 4.0.0 -->

pass_condition: Agent confirms the internal starter has been rebuilt for Boot 4 with a separate artifact version, verifies its dependency tree doesn't pull in Boot 3 internals, and proceeds.

constraint: Annotation processors and codegen plugins need compatibility check (CHECKLIST-DEP-05)

scenario.title: MapStruct annotation processor version incompatible with Boot 4
scenario.user_request: Upgrade to Boot 4 but we still use MapStruct for code generation.
scenario.code_context:
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
    <configuration>
        <annotationProcessorPaths>
            <path>
                <groupId>org.mapstruct</groupId>
                <artifactId>mapstruct-processor</artifactId>
                <version>1.5.5.Final</version>
            </path>
            <path>
                <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                <version>1.18.30</version>
            </path>
        </annotationProcessorPaths>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

pass_condition: Agent flags MapStruct 1.5.5.Final and Lombok 1.18.30 for compatibility review against Boot 4's Java 17+ baseline, checks for known incompatibilities, and recommends upgrading to versions that support the target Java and Boot 4 stack.

constraint: Do not leave properties migrator in final state (MUST-NOT-02)

scenario.title: Both properties migrator and classic test starter left after 'completion'
scenario.user_request: Migration is done. All tests pass. Can we ship it?
scenario.code_context:
<dependencies>
    <!-- Leftover from migration discovery phase -->
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-properties-migrator</artifactId>
        <scope>runtime</scope>
    </dependency>
    <!-- Classic test starter not migrated to slice starters -->
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
        <scope>test</scope>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>

pass_condition: Agent identifies both the properties migrator and the classic test starter as leftover temporary dependencies that must be removed before the migration is considered complete.
